content.question: What is the political and legal process by which the Hungarian government could negotiate with church-maintained schools to align their admissions criteria with state-level reform, and have prior reform attempts succeeded or failed in securing such alignment?
content.reduction_path: Conduct interviews with representatives from major church education maintainers and the Ministry of Education documenting past negotiation outcomes; review any formal bilateral agreements or regulatory instruments governing state–church school coordination; examine constitutional or statutory provisions regarding the extent to which state education policy can conditionally bind church-maintained schools.
